content: Hierarchical task planning can be repurposed as a structural framework for assembling agent-orchestrated answering ensembles, where high-level planners decompose a complex query into sub-questions that are routed to specialized answering agents at each layer of the hierarchy, rather than uniformly pooling all agent responses at once. This layered, task-decomposed ensemble approach outperforms flat answer pooling by matching question complexity to agent capability at each hierarchical level.
